taskDescription: Drag the correct embryological terms into the blanks to complete these statements about the 3rd to 8th week of development.
textField:
The *primitive streak* appears on Day 14 in the caudal midline, establishing the body axes of the embryo.

Gastrulation converts the bilaminar disc into a *trilaminar* disc consisting of ectoderm, *mesoderm*, and endoderm.

The organiser of the entire embryo is the *primitive node*, located at the cranial end of the primitive streak.

If the primitive streak fails to regress, remnant cells in the sacrococcygeal region can form a *sacrococcygeal teratoma*.

The *notochord* induces the overlying ectoderm to form the *neural plate*, which is the first step of neurulation.

The neural plate folds to form the *neural tube*, and failure of closure at the cranial end results in *anencephaly*.

The *somites* are segmented blocks of mesoderm that give rise to the vertebral column, skeletal muscles, and dermis.

The embryonic period ends at the end of the *8th* week, after which the developing human is called a *fetus*.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the embryological development material and try again.
Good effort! Review the concepts you missed.
Excellent! You have a strong grasp of early embryological development.
